description_long: This is a hand-painted ceramic vase with a bulbous body, a narrow neck, and a flared rim. The surface is predominantly white with a gradient to dark gray at the neck and rim, and it features colorful floral motifs in red, yellow, and green, painted in a loose, expressive style. The handle is twisted and painted with alternating yellow and green stripes, adding a tactile and visual accent. The rim appears slightly irregular, giving the vase a rustic, artisanal character. The body is decorated with large, stylized flowers and leaves, which wrap around the circumference. The overall finish is matte with some visible surface texture, suggesting it is crafted from unglazed or lightly glazed ceramic.
description: A hand-painted ceramic vase with a twisted handle, floral motifs in red, yellow, and green, and a matte, textured finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared rim, twisted handle, and a yellow-green floral motif with a red flower on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a large red flower and the transition from white to gray at the neck.
description_view_2: Back view with the twisted handle most prominent and two large red flowers with yellow accents visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle from a different angle and a yellow floral motif with a red bud.
